# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./

RUN npm ci --only=production --ignore-scripts && \
    npm cache clean --force

COPY . .

RUN npm run build

FROM node:18-alpine

RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs && \
    adduser -S reactapp -u 1001 -G nodejs

WORKDIR /app

RUN npm install -g serve@14.2.1 && \
    npm cache clean --force

COPY --from=builder --chown=reactapp:nodejs /app/build ./build

USER reactapp

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["serve", "-s", "build", "-l", "3000", "--no-clipboard"]
